graph_interpretation.title: Multistation synchrony
graph_interpretation.reading: The synchrony CSV shows that up to 3 of 5 stations were simultaneously active. The maximum active fraction is 0.6 (60.0%), whereas the mean active fraction is 0.017475 (1.7475%). Synchronized samples represent 0.6921% of the analyzed window, with a maximum synchronized run of 3 sample(s). The temporal structure is classified as 'sparse synchronization episodes'.

graph_interpretation.title: Station-resolved z-scores
graph_interpretation.key_message: The global z-score distribution remains mostly near background.
graph_interpretation.reading: The allstations metrics CSV contains 432000 station-time rows. The mean |z| is 0.796052, median |z| is 0.68925, p95 |z| is 1.838366, p99 |z| is 2.826147, and max |z| is 6.634788. Using p95 |z|, the global anomaly level is classified as 'low'.

graph_interpretation.title: Extreme anomaly distribution
graph_interpretation.key_message: Extremes are strongly concentrated in time.
graph_interpretation.reading: The allstations scan CSV identifies 12168 extreme samples. The maximum number of extremes in a 30-minute bin is 578, with extremes occupying 48 30-minute bin(s). The temporal pattern is classified as 'strongly clustered extremes'.
graph_interpretation.caveat: Temporal clustering of extremes is meaningful only when read together with synchrony and z-score summaries.

graph_interpretation.title: Anomaly vs synthetic tidal forcing
graph_interpretation.key_message: The anomaly shows moderate linear tracking of the forcing reference.
graph_interpretation.reading: The forcing CSV was read as a contextual reference. The strongest absolute correlation between candidate anomaly columns and forcing-like columns is 0.525505. The relationship is classified as 'moderate_linear_tracking'. This curve is treated as a smooth reference, not as a prediction signal.
graph_interpretation.caveat: The forcing curve is contextual only. It should not be interpreted as a forecasting input or causal proof.

graph_interpretation.title: Robustness / null-model comparison
graph_interpretation.key_message: The robust layer is broadly consistent with null expectations.
graph_interpretation.reading: The robust precursor layer was evaluated from available CSV/JSON outputs. The statistical support is classified as 'consistent_with_null'. The strongest detected robust value is 30.0, and the strongest null-reference value is 499.0.
graph_interpretation.caveat: This is a descriptive robustness check. It does not convert the monitor into a predictive or warning system.

joint_interpretation.title: Joint network interpretation
joint_interpretation.analysis_type: Joint multistation coherence / z-score / extreme / forcing / robust-null reading
evidence: Synchrony reaches 3 of 5 stations at peak, but synchronized samples represent 0.6921% of the window.
The z-score distribution has p95 |z| = 1.838366 and max |z| = 6.634788.
The extreme scan contains 12168 extremes, with up to 578 extremes in a 30-minute bin.
The forcing-reference layer shows moderate_linear_tracking with the anomaly candidates (max |corr| = 0.525505).
The robust/null layer is classified as consistent_with_null (robust/null ratio = 0.06012).
cross_plot_consistency: Extremes are temporally clustered even though the global z-score distribution remains mostly low.
joint_interpretation.summary: Alaska Aleutian Subduction Zone USA shows moderate network structure with clustered extremes. Synchrony and extreme timing suggest episodic structure, but the global z-score distribution remains low. This should be read as a moderate descriptive pattern, not as a sustained network-wide anomaly.

interpretation_logic.fusion_method: rule-based multivariate interpretation from CSV/JSON pipeline outputs

joint_interpretation.score_notice: This is a descriptive structure score for UI ranking only. It is not seismic risk, probability, forecast or warning.
joint_interpretation.notice: Descriptive network-level analysis only. Not predictive. Not an early-warning or risk-assessment system.
